title: The same, but different | Nature Genetics

# The same, but different

* Dorret Boomsma <sup>1</sup>

_Nature Genetics_ **volume 38** , page 735 ( 2006 ) Cite this article

* 623 Accesses
* 2 Citations
* Metrics details

Buy or subscribe

## No Two Alike: Human Nature and Human Individuality

* _Judith Harris_

W.W. Norton, 2006 352 pp., hardcover, $26.95 ISBN 0393059480 | ISBN: 0-393-05948-0

Not every complex trait is heritable, and high heritability does not equal high predictability. The discordance of genetic clones is the focus of Judith Harris's second book, _No Two Alike_ . Why do monozygotic twins differ in behavior and personality (and, one might add, in their risk of developing schizophrenia, other psychopathologies, or cardiovascular disease)?

This is a preview of subscription content, [access via your institution](https://wayf.springernature.com?redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nature.com%2Farticles%2Fng0706-735)
